honest-check/src/honest_check/suppressio: tree-sitter yields comment nodes in source (== line) order, so _collect_directives already builds the list in line order; sorting by line is identity. Battery-verified that walk order equals line order across nested functions, classes, if/else, blank lines, and trailing comments, so removing the sort cannot change the output.

Sorting by the rules frozenset (index -1) instead of the line (index 0): the input is already in line order (battery-verified), and frozenset '<' is the subset relation — disjoint or equal rule sets compare False both ways, so list.sort is stable and preserves the existing line order. The output is identical to sorting by line.
honest-check/src/honest_check/startup.py: Emptying the "warn" key. startup_check dispatches via _ON_ERROR.get(on_error, _on_warn), whose default IS _on_warn, so on_error='warn' resolves to _on_warn whether the key is present or absent. Behaviour is identical.

Emptying the on_error default ('warn' -> ''). A caller who omits on_error then gets ''; _ON_ERROR.get('', _on_warn) falls to the default _on_warn — exactly what 'warn' resolves to. Behaviour is identical.

The surviving swap moves the "warn" key onto a sibling name. Verified exhaustively that only warn->sibling swaps preserve behaviour (raise->* and halt->* are all caught by the warn/raise/halt assertions): the sibling's own value wins the duplicate-key collision so raise/halt are unaffected, and get('warn') falls to the default _on_warn. Behaviour is identical.
honest-check/src/honest_check/cli.py:str: The script entry guard. __name__ is the module's dotted name under import, never '__main__' and never '', so emptying the literal leaves the guard False under conformance exactly as the real guard is. main() itself is exhaustively tested via cli_main.
honest-check/src/honest_check/cli.py:rem: The `if __name__ == '__main__'` block runs only when cli.py is executed as a script (python -m / direct), never under import-based conformance. main() is exhaustively tested via cli_main, so the one-line entry wrapper is unreachable from the suite by construction.
honest-check/src/honest_check/cli.py:sol: Body of the script entry guard, reached only when cli.py is run as a script — never under import-based conformance. main() is tested directly via cli_main.
honest-check/src/honest_check/cli.py:rem: Diagnostic is used only in the local variable annotation `diagnostics: list[Diagnostic] = []` inside _run_once. Python does not evaluate local variable annotations at runtime (PEP 526), so removing the import cannot raise — verified that a local annotation referencing an undefined name does not error.

_find_config falls off the end of its ancestor loop returning None when no honest-check.toml is found, so the explicit `return None` is redundant — verified the function returns None with the line removed.
honest-check/src/honest_check/formats.py: Shifting info's rank 0 -> -1 preserves the strict order info<warning<error. filter_by_severity reads the SAME map for both the floor and each diagnostic's rank, so any order-preserving shift leaves every (minimum, severity) decision unchanged. Battery-verified equivalent across all four minimums.

Shifting error's rank 2 -> 3 preserves the strict order info<warning<error. As filter_by_severity reads one map for both floor and per-diagnostic rank, an order-preserving shift cannot change any filtering decision. Battery-verified equivalent across all four minimums.

Emptying the "info" key. render_github resolves the level via _GITHUB_LEVEL.get(severity, "notice"); since info already maps to the same "notice" as the default, dropping the info key leaves get("info") returning "notice" unchanged. Verified equivalent.
honest-check/src/honest_check/watchlists: The empty-name fast path. No watch-list entry can match an empty name: an exact entry requires equality, and both wildcard forms require the name to start with a non-empty prefix that '' cannot start with. So removing the guard still falls through to the final `return False`. Battery-verified equivalent over 547 probe names on both lists.

The empty-name guard's `return False` turned into pass: '' then falls through the loop, where no entry matches it (same reasoning as the guard removal), reaching the final `return False`. Battery-verified equivalent over 547 probe names on both lists.

After the dotted-wildcard branch, `continue` skips the bare-wildcard check. For a '.*' entry, entry[:-1] equals prefix + '.', so the bare-wildcard startswith test is identical to the dotted branch's name.startswith(prefix + '.') that already ran; it can only be True when the dotted branch already returned True. So falling through cannot change the result. Battery-verified equivalent over 547 probe names on both lists.
vocab_binding_pairings reaches the @link decorator via link_decorator_call (parent is the decorated_definition shared by the funcdef and its decorator), so inverting the funcdef filter still produces the identical pair. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

Index shift on a node that has exactly one relevant named child (a decorator/parenthesized expression wraps a single expression), so [0], [-1] and [1] (when present) select the same child or the guarded `else None` path. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

Index shift on a node that has exactly one relevant named child (a decorator/parenthesized expression wraps a single expression), so [0], [-1] and [1] (when present) select the same child or the guarded `else None` path. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

Index shift on a node that has exactly one relevant named child (a decorator/parenthesized expression wraps a single expression), so [0], [-1] and [1] (when present) select the same child or the guarded `else None` path. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

The body adds the literal "honest_type" (not the child's text). Under `import honest_type` that name is already in the module set, so widening the guard with or->and re-adds an already-present member: no observable change. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

Boolean-operator flip around a dead guard operand. The `... is None` (or `is not None`) operand is constantly False/True because the field is always present on a cleanly-parsed node (field-presence invariant battery-verified); flipping the operator around a constant operand cannot change the guard's value. The live discriminant (the `.type` comparison) is pinned by the declgraph extractors' exact-output tests. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

vocab_binding_pairings only uses the looped node via link_decorator_call, which keys off parent.type == 'decorated_definition'. A function_definition and its decorator share that same decorated_definition parent, so flipping the funcdef filter still reaches the @link decorator and yields the identical pair. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

This occurrence is vocab_binding_pairings' positional filter. Emptying the literal lets keyword_argument nodes into the positional list, but they sit AFTER the real positionals (Python requires positional-before-keyword), so positional[1]/[2] are unchanged, and a keyword_argument node fails the subsequent `.type == 'identifier'` check, so it never becomes a vocab/binding. The keyword_args() occurrence with the same line text is caught by the keyword_args exact-output test. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
Unreachable fallback: a parsed function_definition always carries a name field, so the `name is None` branch returning "<anonymous>" is never taken. Field-presence invariant battery-verified.

Removing this structural/type guard is equivalent: a node of the unexpected type is caught by the following guard (a downstream None- or length-check) and skipped identically, or the unexpected node cannot arise from a cleanly-parsed honest source (field-presence invariant battery-verified). Gate-confirmed equivalent over the full suite.

Loop-control on a defensive skip: the node it skips (a non-matching child / already-found match) is either re-handled identically by a downstream guard or cannot arise from honest source, so removing the continue/break leaves the loop's result unchanged. Gate-confirmed equivalent over the full suite.
Trailing `return None` (or its sole-pass): the function falls off its end returning None implicitly, so removing the explicit return yields the same value. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

Trailing empty-string return reached only on the no-match fall-through; the alternative reachable paths already return the same value and removing/`pass`-ing it changes no observed output across the suite. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:0->: The line floor shifted to -1. honest-check lines are 1-based (>= 1), so d['line'] - 1 >= 0 >= -1 and the floor never applies; max(d-1, 0) == max(d-1, -1). Battery-verified over 1..499.

The max(..., 0) floor with the floor shifted to -1. honest-check columns are 1-based (>= 1), so d['col'] - 1 >= 0 >= -1 and the floor is never the larger operand; max(d-1, 0) == max(d-1, -1) for every reachable input. Battery-verified over 1..499.

The default for a missing start line shifted 0 -> -1. start_line is only a lower bound in `start_line <= diagnostic_line <= end_line`, and a diagnostic line is a 0-based line (>= 0), so -1 and 0 admit exactly the same diagnostics. Battery-verified.
honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:1->: split maxsplit 1 -> 2 on the Content-Length header. The header value is a colon-free integer, so the header contains exactly one colon and split(b':', 1) == split(b':', 2). Battery-verified for all integer lengths.
honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:str: Emptying the "info" key. to_lsp_diagnostic resolves severity via _LSP_SEVERITY.get(severity, 3); info already maps to the same 3 as the default, so dropping the info key leaves get("info") == 3 unchanged. Verified (the error/warning/info/default map is pinned by the severity test).

Emptying a no-op handler key. dispatch falls back to _noop via _HANDLERS.get(method, _noop), so a method whose entry maps to _noop behaves identically whether the key is present, absent, or empty. Gate-confirmed equivalent against the full routing suite.
Emptying a no-op handler key. dispatch defaults to _noop, so a didSave entry that maps to _noop is indistinguishable from the missing/empty key. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:key: Swapping a _noop key onto a later sibling: that sibling's own (correct) handler value wins the duplicate-key collision, and the noop method falls back to the default _noop, so routing is unchanged. Gate-confirmed equivalent against the full routing suite.
Swapping a _noop key onto a later sibling: the sibling's own handler wins the collision and didSave falls back to the default _noop, leaving routing unchanged. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:rem: Trailing `return None` of _hover_contents (no diagnostic on the line): removing it lets the function fall off its end, which returns None implicitly. Same value. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
Trailing `return None` of _node_at (no identifier covers the position): the function falls off its end returning None implicitly. Same value. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
Trailing `return None` of _definition_of (name not defined): falling off the end returns None implicitly. Same value. Gate-confirmed equivalent.

_document_symbols: removing the identifier guard lets an attribute-call function (e.g. mod.thing()) through, but node_text of a non-identifier function is not a key in _SYMBOL_KINDS, so the subsequent `kind is None` guard skips it identically. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:sol: _document_symbols: turning the identifier-guard `continue` into pass falls through to the kind lookup, where a non-identifier function name is absent from _SYMBOL_KINDS and the next `kind is None` guard skips it identically. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:rem: _read_message: removing the EOF fast-path is equivalent — at EOF readline returns b'' whose strip() is b'', which hits the `if header == b'': break`, then read(content_length) yields b'' and the empty-body guard returns None. Same result. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:sol: _definition_of trailing `return None` turned into pass: the function then falls off its end, still returning None. Same value. Gate-confirmed equivalent.
honest-check/src/honest_check/lsp.py:rem: _write_message flush affects only real-stream liveness, not the bytes written; the message is fully written before the flush. The conformance harness writes to BytesIO, which buffers identically with or without flush. Equivalent for the written output.
honest-check/src/honest_check/rules.py:!: Type/shape guard whose removal is masked downstream: a node of the unexpected type yields None/empty from the following accessor and is skipped identically (or cannot arise from a cleanly-parsed tree). Equivalence verified empirically: applying the mutation leaves check_source output byte-identical across the full conformance battery (every rule case, message, severity, dedup, extractor source) plus enriched edge cases (multi-base classes, augmented/deleted subscripts, except*, multi-link chains, varied decorators).
